texto: England is seen at its worst when it has to deal with men like Wilde. In Germany Wilde and Byron are appreciated as authors: in England they still go pecking about their love-affairs. Anyone who calls a book ‘immoral’ or 'moral’ should be caned. A book by itself can be neither. It is only a question of the morality or immorality of the reader. But the English approach all questions of vice with such a curious mixture of curiosity and fear that it’s impossible to deal with them.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Charles Hamilton Sorley: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “England is seen at its worst when it has to deal with men like Wilde. In Germany Wilde and Byron …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, george-gordon-byron, lord-byron pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de lord-byron e oscar-wilde.
